scene.title: Referrals/week before the fall recruiting cycle
scene.summary: A consulting-recruiting club where weekly coffee-chats and referral DMs are logged.

mcq_options.text: Take the midpoint of the baseline commitment and the group's weighted average, ignoring how sensitive this person is to peers — near 30.3 chats.
Anchor on the middle classmate of the group and scale up the private floor by that median pull — arrives near 54.3 chats.
Use the most tight-knit classmate's move as the only relevant pull above the baseline need — the weekly coffee chats ends up around 59.1 chats.
Combine the baseline commitment with a closeness-weighted blend of what the classmates do, so familiar voices count more than distant ones — ends up near 57.3 chats.

cot_text: Reasoning: Before tagging, rule out the alternatives: this scene is not non-positional — study hours or practice counts are pinned in a shared channel the cohort reads. It is also not positional-consumption — the visible signal is hours-of-work or skill marks, not a consumption bill. What remains and fits the observable cues around the protagonist is positional-human-capital, so the scene-type flag is positional-human-capital. Within this category, the commitment is lifted above the private baseline by a closeness-weighted reference drawn from the classmates. The close-in-life relationships carry most of the weight; the more peripheral ones barely register. The comparison-sensitivity of the protagonist scales that aggregate pull before it stacks on top of the baseline floor. The implied commitment is the private floor plus the closeness-weighted peer reference, landing near 57.3 chats.
